title: Tennessee railway conductor fired over viral July 4 message targeting non-Americans: 'If you disagree, you can leave'
summary: "To the very, very few Americans in here, Happy Independence Day,” Jack Peterson, the operator, told his passengers to the 131-year-old tourist attraction Incline Railway in Tennessee on July 4. "To the rest of you, welcome to the greatest country on the face of the planet, and if you disagree, you can leave," Peterson said.
imageAlt: Tennessee railway conductor fired over viral July 4 message targeting non-Americans: 'If you disagree, you can leave'
